item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree that this is underdeveloped. I feel like it could be improved by the inclusion of whoever (or preferably whatever) hunts these Tooth Striders in action.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is under-developed. The nocturnal hunting teeth is a concept I could get behind, anything with teeth creeps me the fuck out, but the description of their functionality is almost perfunctory and definitely contrived.

The teeth are described as holding people down with their roots, but I don't see how six teeth and their roots could hold someone down. There's the potential there for some Gulliver's Travels style interactions between the teeth and the person they're holding down but we don't even get a good idea of how they do it let alone the methods they would use or how they would respond to a person waking up or anything at all.

It ends up just being half a dozen teeth who jump into peoples mouths and suck out the bones. Which doesn't sound bad on the face of it, but that's all they do.

The note at the end is bland and incongruous with the rest of the piece. They're supposed to be these predatory entities but they're also organized with an agenda and have the resources to print flyers? The description makes them seem almost like animals shaped like teeth so I don't see it.

All of the individual pieces here of the article have the potential to be cool. Evil teeth crawling in your mouth to get big and then… the article doesn't really answer the greater motive or give the reader much of reason to speculate about one, actually.

This is boring and squanders a lot of potential to be really good classic-style body horror. It does the least possible with the idea, throws in a note at the end and that's what we get. I don't like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, honestly. Classic body horror and suitably weird initial discovery. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe in the themes and details, but the overall structure and conceit would be identical if it wasn't better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as I'm concerned, that article shares a lot of themes and elements with this one despite me having never read that before. Plus, I'm fairly certain that there's a fair difference in that article to separate it from my own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly the conclusion I came to here.

item.title: Toothpost 2.0
item.content:encoded:
This didn't do well the first time. Hopefully it works now.

Big thanks to Nagiros, Cerastes, and Deadly Bread for their crit.

And extra special-sauce-tier thanks to The Great Hippo for not only his critique, but for his massive overhauling of my original description.

Sources:
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Weisheitszahn1.JPG
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cavity.jpg
